bibliographic_data.title: Rescattering of non-minimal coupling scalar particles during inflation
bibliographic_data.abstract: We investigate the rescattering effects arising from non-minimally coupled scalar particles χ that are suddenly produced during inflation. The coupling term ξRχ2 significantly enhances resonant particle production compared to minimal coupling scenarios. Consequently, the produced χ particles rescattering off the homogeneous inflaton condensate ϕ, generating abundant δϕ quanta within very short time intervals. This process leads to characteristic enhancements in the power spectrum of primordial curvature perturbations at scales corresponding to the moments of particle production. When this occurs at small scales, the power spectrum amplitude can reach as high as O(10−2). Furthermore, analysis of the equilateral bispectrum shows that this mechanism also induces substantial non-Gaussian features.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper explores how rescattering of non-minimally coupled scalar particles leads to corrections in the power spectrum of primordial curvature perturbations and induces distinctive equilateral-type bispectra.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors use a non-minimal coupling model describing sudden particle production during inflation, analyze its rescattering imprints on curvature perturbations using Bogoliubov coefficient formalism and in-in formalism, and investigate the induced non-Gaussianities by computing the equilateral bispectrum and evaluating the fNL parameter over relevant scales.
outcomes: Enhancements in the power spectrum of primordial curvature perturbations at small scales up to O(10−2)
Substantial non-Gaussian features characterized by fNL ∼O(1000)
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper investigates how rescattering of non-minimally coupled scalar particles leads to corrections in the power spectrum of primordial curvature perturbations and induces distinctive equilateral-type bispectra. The authors use a non-minimal coupling model describing sudden particle production during inflation, analyze its rescattering imprints on curvature perturbations using Bogoliubov coefficient formalism and in-in formalism, and investigate the induced non-Gaussianities by computing the equilateral bispectrum and evaluating the fNL parameter over relevant scales. The study finds that when this occurs at small scales, the power spectrum amplitude can reach as high as O(10−2), and substantial non-Gaussian features characterized by fNL ∼O(1000) are induced.
